stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/AI domains such as transformers and text-generation.
The presence of 'safetensors' indicates modern, secure model handling practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies including transformers, safetensors, and text-generation.
The focus is on a specific, powerful instruction-tuned language model from Meta.

stories.title: NYT slams Microsoft for building copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The New York Times is reportedly shifting its copyright claims regarding OpenAI and Microsoft.
The shift is linked to a recent Supreme Court ruling that was unfavorable to Sony in copyright matters.
The core issue involves allegations that Microsoft built a supercomputer that infringes on copyrights used by OpenAI.

stories.title: Anonymous GitHub account mass-dropping undisclosed 0-days
stories.author: binyu
bullet_points: Anonymous actor released undisclosed zero-day vulnerabilities.
The vulnerabilities were dropped via a mass upload to GitHub.
This indicates a significant security threat landscape event.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face platform.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components such as transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher quality outputs, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, offering faster local AI inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is running AI capabilities entirely on the user's own device.

stories.title: Apple wants permission to buy memory from a blacklisted Chinese supplier
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Apple is attempting to secure an exception from the Trump administration to purchase RAM chips.
The supplier, CXMT, is blacklisted by the Pentagon due to alleged ties to the People's Liberation Army.
The move is driven by the rising costs of RAM and storage components in Apple's supply chain.

stories.title: South Korea plans to train entire military as "drone warriors"
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: South Korea is planning a major military overhaul focusing on drone integration.
The entire half-million-strong military will receive training in drone operations.
Drones are being designated as a 'universal combat tool' in their military strategy.

stories.title: When Does Combining Language Models Help? A Co-Failure Ceiling on Routing, Voting, and Mixture-of-Agents Across 67 Frontier Models
stories.author: Josef Chen
bullet_points: The paper analyzes the performance ceiling of multi-model LLM systems (routing, voting, etc.) designed to improve accuracy over single models.
It introduces a theoretical limit: the accuracy gain is capped by $1 - \beta$, where $\beta$ is the rate at which all constituent models fail on the same query.
The study highlights a limitation in current diagnostic methods, noting that standard error correlation ($\rho$) cannot reliably identify this fundamental failure rate ($\beta$).

stories.title: Prompt Injection in Automated Résumé Screening with Large Language Models: Single and Multi-Injection Settings
stories.author: Stefanus Jasin
bullet_points: The research investigates prompt injection vulnerabilities in LLM-based automated résumé screening systems.
Prompt injection is defined as subtle, self-promotional text designed to manipulate LLM evaluations without adding new qualifications.
Experiments demonstrate that prompt injection reliably improves applicant rankings, especially when résumé quality is uniform.

stories.title: Reinforcement Learning without Ground-Truth Solutions can Improve LLMs
stories.author: Yuxiong He
bullet_points: Introduces RiVER, a framework for training Large Language Models (LLMs) using Reinforcement Learning without relying on ground-truth answers.
RiVER utilizes deterministic execution feedback as continuous-valued supervision for score-based optimization tasks.
This addresses a major limitation in current RL-based LLM training, expanding applicability to tasks where solutions are unknown.

stories.title: Hallucination in World Models is Predictable and Preventable
stories.author: Xiaolong Wang
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of 'hallucination' in modern generative world models, where simulations drift from reality.
Proposes that hallucinations are concentrated in low-coverage areas of the state-action space, suggesting a data-centric solution.
Introduces MMBench2, a large-scale dataset designed to benchmark and test visual world modeling capabilities.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for better performance on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The key feature is that these agents run locally on the user's own hardware, emphasizing privacy.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Inside the room where the smart home industry is still betting on Matter
stories.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy
bullet_points: Matter is an interoperability standard designed to unify the fragmented smart home industry.
The standard was a collaborative effort involving major tech rivals like Apple, Google, and Amazon.
The article focuses on the industry's continued reliance and betting on Matter's success.

stories.title: Antibiotic "megacluster" discovery provides new strategy to fight superbugs
stories.author: Beth Mole
bullet_points: A new antibiotic 'megacluster' has been discovered.
This discovery offers a novel strategy against antibiotic-resistant superbugs.
The finding is viewed as a significant step in replenishing the global antibiotic supply.

stories.title: Language-Based Digital Twins for Elderly Cognitive Assistance
stories.author: Hiroko H. Dodge
bullet_points: Proposes a language-based digital twin framework for personalized healthcare.
Utilizes Large Language Models (LLMs) to model the conversational behavior of elderly individuals.
Aims to provide non-invasive early detection of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) using language patterns.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses the weak planning and limited generalization of small, cost-efficient MLLMs used in GUI agents.
A new method, PEEU (Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization), is introduced to enhance task planning for multimodal web agents.
The goal is to enable autonomous exploration for complex, repetitive GUI tasks, balancing efficiency with advanced planning capabilities.

stories.title: AI Healthcare Chatbots as Information Infrastructure: A Large-Scale Study of User-Reported Breakdowns
stories.author: Masooda Bashir
bullet_points: The study analyzes over 15,000 user reviews across 59 AI healthcare chatbots to assess real-world performance.
Three primary recurring breakdowns were identified: access barriers/service unreliability, and user experience/interaction quality.
The research addresses the growing need to study the functional and emotional impact of AI chatbots in health information seeking.

stories.title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of unifying diverse capabilities (T2I, local/global editing) in modern image generation models.
Identifies a core problem: editing capabilities often conflict with or degrade base text-to-image performance.
Introduces 'DanceOPD' as a novel method to effectively compose and align these conflicting image generation functionalities.

stories.title: When are likely answers right? On Sequence Probability and Correctness in LLMs
stories.author: Jonas Geiping
bullet_points: The paper investigates the fundamental relationship between sequence probability and factual correctness in Large Language Models (LLMs).
It analyzes how different decoding methods shift probability mass to generate outputs, questioning when this probabilistic likelihood equates to being 'right'.
The research aims to quantify this alignment across various LLM models and decoding strategies.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses limitations in small, cost-efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent task planning.
It introduces the Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU) method to improve autonomous exploration and task decomposition.
The work aims to enhance the ability of open-source agents to handle complex, repetitive GUI tasks across different websites.

stories.title: DanceOPD: On-Policy Generative Field Distillation
stories.author: Tat-Seng Chua
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of unifying diverse capabilities (T2I, local/global editing) in modern image generation models.
Identifies that existing capabilities often conflict, with editing degrading T2I performance and editing methods interfering with each other.
Introduces 'DanceOPD' as a new method designed to effectively compose these conflicting image generation capabilities.

stories.title: Empowering GUI Agents via Autonomous Experience Exploration and Hindsight Experience Utilization for Task Planning
stories.author: Jun Zhao
bullet_points: The research addresses the weak planning and limited generalization of small, cost-efficient Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) used for GUI agent tasks.
It introduces the Planning Experience Exploration and Utilization (PEEU) method to improve task planning for web agents.
The goal is to enable autonomous exploration and effective decomposition of complex, repetitive GUI tasks using smaller, privacy-preserving models.

stories.title: Beyond Surface Forms: A Comprehensive, Mechanism-Oriented Taxonomy of Indirect Linguistic Encoding for LLM-Based Coded Language Detection
stories.author: Paul Jen-Hwa Hu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the problem of indirect linguistic encoding (ILE) used by users to evade social media moderation and surveillance.
It proposes a mechanism-oriented taxonomy to categorize these evasive language techniques (like algospeak and euphemisms) based on underlying operations, rather than communicative goals.
This research is highly relevant to the development of more robust and sophisticated detection models for Large Language Models (LLMs).

stories.title: How Surprising Is Historical Italian to Language Models? Tokenization Tax, Comprehension Tax, and a Simple Mitigation
stories.author: Maria Levchenko
bullet_points: The paper addresses the poorly understood challenge of using LLMs on historical languages, specifically Italian.
It proposes a diagnostic framework to break down historical language difficulty into four components, including tokenization cost and predictive uncertainty.
The research aims to move beyond treating historical language difficulty as a single, monolithic barrier to improve LLM performance in digital library workflows.

stories.title: LMs as Task-Specific Knowledge Bases: An Interpretability Analysis
stories.author: Mor Geva
bullet_points: The paper investigates the hypothesis that Language Models (LMs) function as task-specific knowledge bases.
It examines whether LMs maintain the consistency property of traditional knowledge bases (i.e., returning the same fact regardless of the query phrasing).
The findings suggest that LMs encode factual knowledge in a manner that is dependent on the specific task being performed.

stories.title: AI Is Designing Radio Chips That Humans Couldn't Even Imagine
stories.author: Brajeshwar
bullet_points: AI is being used to design complex radio chips.
The designs generated by AI are beyond human imagination.
This indicates a significant advancement in AI-driven hardware co-design.

stories.title: 5 Things Your LLM Bill Is Hiding From You (And How to Find Them)
stories.author: arpitstack
bullet_points: The article addresses unexpected and rapid increases in LLM operational costs.
It highlights a scenario where costs spiked significantly without corresponding increases in usage or errors.
The content promises actionable advice on methods to audit and uncover hidden LLM billing charges.

stories.title: Constitution > Prompts: How We Govern 9 Autonomous Agents Without a Central Orchestrator
stories.author: zwiserfit
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of unconstrained AI agent loops leading to potential system failures.
Presents a novel architectural approach for governing multiple autonomous agents without relying on a central orchestrator.
Focuses on practical, real-world application challenges in running AI agents within a business context.

stories.title: Katra: Giving AI Agents a Vulcan Mind Meld
stories.author: kai_polyquant_v2
bullet_points: Katra is developing cognitive memory infrastructure for AI agents.
The system focuses on enabling agents to remember and reflect on past interactions.
The technology aims to give AI agents a more advanced, 'Vulcan-like' level of cognitive ability.
